summary: Iteration 8 — Shopify-style Checkout aesthetic refactor. Both backend regression and frontend visual checks PASS. Backend test_iteration5.py: 10/10 PASS (settings shape, offers single/couple/gift, payment_options, product main_image reachable, admin login token, /, /product, /checkout SPA, POST /api/orders cod 200, main_image content-type=image). Frontend Playwright at 420x1100 on /checkout: (1) 'Secure Checkout' section header present with long reassurance subtitle 'All transactions are secure and encrypted. Your order includes free returns and 24/7 access to our award-winning customer service.' (visible in screenshot), (2) CardBrandBadges render VISA (#1A1F71), Mastercard dual-circle, AMEX (#2E77BB) + '+ more' to the right of the Card row title, (3) clicking pm-card sets payment_method='card' and the row gets `rounded-xl border-2 ... bg-white border-[#202223]` (NO amber bg, NO amber border), (4) clicking pm-cod swaps active dark border to COD and Card row falls back to border-[#E1E3E5] (true radio behavior), (5) Pay-now button classes are exactly `bg-[#202223] hover:bg-black text-white`, text reads 'Pay now · $34.99' (no amber/orange), (6) Shipping method 'Standard Delivery' row uses the same dark border-2 + white bg pattern with $5.00 shipping (below the $50 free-ship threshold; FREE accent path is in the same JSX so it'll render green-600 when discounted>=threshold), (7) Dynamic card-row sub-fields render correctly under the active Card row — Billing email + Billing phone fields visible (same render path as admin-configured card_extra_fields). The PayPal button is also re-themed to PayPal yellow #FFC439.
critical_code_review_comments: Checkout.jsx PaymentRow refactor is clean: active state now reduces to a single semantic prop (`border-[#202223]` vs `border-[#E1E3E5]`) — the amber tint and amber-50 bg are fully removed, matching the Shopify reference.

CardBrandBadges is a self-contained presentational component with hard-coded brand colors — acceptable, but consider exposing it as a shared component if you start using it on other pages (Cart drawer, Account billing tab).

PayPal 'Express checkout' button (line 571) and the in-row 'Continue with PayPal' button (line 773) both correctly use #FFC439; nice consistency. Minor nit: the in-row button uses #F2BA36 on hover while the express button uses #F1B82E — pick one for full visual consistency.

Pre-existing carry-over (not in this iteration's scope): server.py is ~765 lines; PUT /api/admin/settings is still full-replace; Checkout.jsx is now ~855 lines and could be split (PaymentSection, DeliverySection, OrderSummary already extracted internally — promoting them to separate files would help).
test_credentials: admin@glowcamp.com / GlowCamp@2026 — validated live via test_iteration5.py.
seed_data_creation: test_iteration5.py creates one TEST_ prefixed cod order (full_name='TEST_Smoke Buyer'). No new data from Playwright (cart was seeded via localStorage; no order submitted).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Checkout visual refactor is locked in: dark border-2 + white bg for active payment rows, card brand badges on Card row only, 'Secure Checkout' label + long subtitle, dark Pay button, PayPal yellow buttons. test_iteration5.py remains the canonical backend smoke. To exercise dynamic card_extra_fields end-to-end (PUT /api/settings then GET on the Card row), authenticate as admin, PUT a transient field into settings.card_extra_fields, reload /checkout, and verify FloatInput renders with the configured label/format. The two billing sub-fields (billing_email, billing_phone) already exercise the same render path and were verified rendering in this iteration.
rca of the issue: Not a bug — purely a visual CSS-class refactor on /checkout. Backend APIs and React state shape are unchanged; only border/bg classes on PaymentRow, an added CardBrandBadges component, the Section title/subtitle text, the shipping-method row classes, and the Pay-now/PayPal button colors were modified.
